contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to rituximab, murine proteins, or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of fatal infusion reactions, anaphylaxis, and serum sickness.
B-cell depletion impairs immune response, potentially resulting in overwhelming sepsis.
Re-exposure can be fatal; monitor for JC virus reactivation.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hepatitis B virus reactivation risk (HBsAg positive or HBcAb positive)
contraindications.reason: High risk of fulminant hepatitis, hepatic failure, and death from HBV reactivation.

contraindications.contraindication_name: severe heart failure (NYHA class IV) or uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias
contraindications.reason: Infusion reactions can precipitate cardiac events.
Risk of disseminated infection from vaccine strain.
B-cell depletion in fetus/neonate; avoid pregnancy for 12 months post-treatment.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to upadacitinib or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis and serious skin reactions.
JAK inhibition increases risk of TB reactivation and progression.
Impaired host defense can lead to sepsis and life-threatening outcomes.
Significantly increased drug exposure and hepatotoxicity risk.

contraindications.contraindication_name: absolute lymphocyte count <500 cells/mm³, ANC <1000 cells/mm³, or hemoglobin <8 g/dL
contraindications.reason: Severe myelosuppression increases risk of infection and anemia.
JAK inhibitors may promote tumor growth; risk-benefit must be assessed.
Increased risk of DVT and PE, particularly in patients with risk factors.
Increased risk of MACE in patients >50 years with CV risk factors.
Risk of disseminated infection from vaccine strain.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with strong CYP3A4 inducers
contraindications.reason: Reduced upadacitinib exposure, leading to loss of efficacy.
Animal studies suggest teratogenicity; human data lacking.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to tofacitinib or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis.
JAK inhibition increases risk of TB reactivation.
Impaired immune response to severe infections.
Significantly increased drug exposure and hepatotoxicity risk.

contraindications.contraindication_name: absolute lymphocyte count <500 cells/mm³, ANC <1000 cells/mm³, or hemoglobin <9 g/dL
contraindications.reason: Severe myelosuppression; starting therapy in this state is contraindicated.
Increased risk of DVT/PE, particularly in higher doses and in patients with risk factors.

contraindications.contraindication_name: major cardiovascular event risk (patients >50 with CV risk factors)
contraindications.reason: Higher rate of MACE observed in safety trials.
Increased risk of malignancies including lymphomas.
Risk of disseminated infection.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with biologic immunosuppressants (TNF inhibitors, IL-6R antagonists)
contraindications.reason: Increased immunosuppression and infection risk.
Category C; animal studies show teratogenicity at high doses.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to tofacitinib or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis.
JAK inhibition increases risk of TB reactivation.
Impaired immune response to severe infections.
Significantly increased drug exposure and hepatotoxicity risk.

contraindications.contraindication_name: absolute lymphocyte count <500 cells/mm³, ANC <1000 cells/mm³, or hemoglobin <9 g/dL
contraindications.reason: Severe myelosuppression contraindicates initiation.
Increased risk of DVT/PE.

contraindications.contraindication_name: major cardiovascular event risk (patients >50 with CV risk factors)
contraindications.reason: Higher rate of MACE observed.
Increased malignancy risk.
Risk of disseminated infection.
Increased immunosuppression risk.
Category C; avoid during pregnancy unless benefit outweighs risk.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to auranofin or other gold compounds
contraindications.reason: Risk of severe anaphylactoid reactions and gold toxicity.
Gold is excreted renally; accumulation can cause nephrotoxicity and membranous glomerulonephritis.
Risk of hepatotoxicity, cholestatic jaundice, and hepatic necrosis.

contraindications.contraindication_name: history of blood dyscrasias (aplastic anemia, agranulocytosis, thrombocytopenia)
contraindications.reason: Gold can cause severe bone marrow suppression; re-challenge is contraindicated.

contraindications.contraindication_name: exfoliative dermatitis or history of severe gold-induced skin reactions
contraindications.reason: Risk of severe mucocutaneous reactions like Stevens-Johnson syndrome.
Category C; known teratogen in animal studies; gold crosses placenta.
Gold is excreted in breast milk; risk of toxicity in nursing infant.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with antimalarials or penicillamine
contraindications.reason: Increased risk of hematologic and renal toxicity.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to sarilumab or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis and serious injection site reactions.
IL-6 inhibition impairs granuloma formation, risking TB reactivation.
Immunosuppression can mask signs of acute inflammation, leading to overwhelming infection.
Increased risk of neutropenia-related infections; do not initiate therapy.
Risk of thrombocytopenia exacerbation.
IL-6 inhibition may alter CYP450 enzymes; increased drug exposure and hepatotoxicity risk.
Increased risk of GI perforation, particularly with concurrent corticosteroids or NSAIDs.
Risk of disseminated infection from vaccine strain.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with other biologic immunosuppressants
contraindications.reason: Increased risk of serious infections.
Monoclonal antibodies cross placenta; limited human data; weigh risk against benefit.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to alemtuzumab or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of fatal infusion reactions, anaphylaxis, and serum sickness.
Profound T-cell and B-cell depletion impairs immune response, risking fatal sepsis.
Risk of PML reactivation and fatal outcome.
Further depletion of CD4+ T-cells can cause severe immunodeficiency.

contraindications.contraindication_name: active hepatitis B or C infection
contraindications.reason: Risk of fulminant hepatitis and hepatic failure.

contraindications.contraindication_name: cerebrovascular event or cervical artery dissection within past 6 months
contraindications.reason: Increased risk of stroke and arterial dissection in MS patients.
Increased risk of bleeding and thrombocytopenia.
Risk of disseminated infection due to profound immunosuppression.
Category C; may cause fetal harm; avoid pregnancy during and for 4 months after treatment.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to camphor, menthol, methyl salicylate, or salicylates
contraindications.reason: Risk of allergic contact dermatitis or, rarely, anaphylaxis.

contraindications.contraindication_name: application to broken, damaged, or irritated skin
contraindications.reason: Enhanced systemic absorption of methyl salicylate can lead to salicylate toxicity.
Heat increases absorption and risk of thermal burns and salicylism.

contraindications.contraindication_name: peptic ulcer disease or bleeding disorders
contraindications.reason: Systemic absorption of salicylate can exacerbate bleeding tendency.
Salicylate metabolites can accumulate, risking nephrotoxicity.
Systemic salicylates can cause premature closure of ductus arteriosus and prolonged labor.
Even topical salicylate absorption can potentiate bleeding risk.
Cross-reactivity risk with penicillin; may cause severe anaphylaxis.
Drug and metabolites accumulate, increasing risk of nephrotoxicity and proteinuria.

contraindications.contraindication_name: history of penicillamine-induced aplastic anemia or agranulocytosis
contraindications.reason: Re-challenge can be fatal due to irreversible bone marrow suppression.
Re-exposure can cause rapid progression to renal failure.
Penicillamine can induce or exacerbate myasthenic syndrome.
Category D; known teratogen causing cutis laxa and congenital defects.
Excreted in breast milk; risk of toxicity in nursing infant.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with gold salts or antimalarials
contraindications.reason: Increased risk of hematologic and dermatologic toxicity.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to etanercept or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis and serious injection site reactions.
TNF-alpha inhibition predisposes to reactivation of latent TB.

contraindications.contraindication_name: active serious infection (sepsis, abscesses, opportunistic infections)
contraindications.reason: Anti-TNF therapy impairs immune response, risking overwhelming infection.
Must screen for and treat latent TB before initiating therapy.

contraindications.contraindication_name: history of hepatitis B virus reactivation risk
contraindications.reason: Reactivation of HBV can occur; screen and monitor carriers.

contraindications.contraindication_name: demyelinating disease (e.g., multiple sclerosis, optic neuritis)
contraindications.reason: May exacerbate or cause new-onset central nervous system demyelinating disorders.
TNF inhibition may worsen cardiac function; use with caution.
Risk of disseminated infection from live vaccine.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with anakinra or abatacept
contraindications.reason: Increased risk of serious infections without proven efficacy benefit.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to interferon gamma-1b or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis and severe systemic allergic reactions.
Interferon gamma can cause hepatotoxicity; risk of hepatic decompensation.
Decreased clearance of interferon; increased risk of systemic toxicity.

contraindications.contraindication_name: history of severe depression or suicidal ideation
contraindications.reason: Interferon gamma can exacerbate neuropsychiatric disorders.

contraindications.contraindication_name: significant cardiac disease (unstable angina, recent MI)
contraindications.reason: Flu-like symptoms and fever may stress the cardiovascular system.
Interferons may cause neurotoxicity and lower seizure threshold.
Interferon gamma can cause or worsen bone marrow suppression.
Category C; may cause fetotoxic effects; use only if benefit justifies risk.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to aripiprazole or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis, angioedema, and severe skin reactions.
Increased risk of cerebrovascular events (stroke, TIA) and mortality.
Significantly reduced clearance; potential for increased CNS depression and toxicity.
Re-challenge can precipitate fatal NMS recurrence.

contraindications.contraindication_name: clinically significant cardiovascular disease (recent MI, unstable angina)
contraindications.reason: Oral aripiprazole prolongs QT interval; parenteral form may increase cardiovascular stress.
Antipsychotics lower seizure threshold; use with extreme caution.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with strong CYP3A4 or CYP2D6 inhibitors
contraindications.reason: Requires dose reduction due to increased aripiprazole exposure.
Category C; neonatal withdrawal syndrome and extrapyramidal symptoms reported.
Excreted in breast milk; potential for infant sedation and feeding difficulties.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to chlorpromazine or any phenothiazine
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis and severe cross-reactivity within class.
Increased risk of cerebrovascular events and mortality.

contraindications.contraindication_name: comatose state or severe CNS depression
contraindications.reason: Additive CNS depression and respiratory depression; may obscure diagnosis.
Anticholinergic effects can precipitate acute angle closure crisis.
Chlorpromazine is hepatically metabolized; accumulation can cause hepatotoxicity and cholestatic jaundice.
Re-challenge can be fatal.
Phenothiazines can cause agranulocytosis and leukopenia; risk of fatal infection.
Anticholinergic effects can cause acute urinary retention.

contraindications.contraindication_name: prolonged QT syndrome or concurrent QT-prolonging agents
contraindications.reason: Chlorpromazine prolongs QT interval; risk of Torsades de Pointes.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with strong CNS depressants (alcohol, opioids)
contraindications.reason: Additive respiratory depression and sedation.
Neonatal withdrawal syndrome and extrapyramidal symptoms possible in third trimester.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to paliperidone, risperidone, or any excipient
contraindications.reason: Risk of anaphylaxis and angioedema; cross-reactive with risperidone.
Increased mortality and cerebrovascular events (stroke, TIA).
Paliperidone minimally metabolized by liver, but hepato-renal syndrome can impair clearance.
Renal excretion is primary; accumulation can lead to toxicity.
Re-challenge can precipitate fatal NMS.

contraindications.contraindication_name: clinically significant cardiac disease (heart failure, arrhythmias)
contraindications.reason: QT prolongation risk; orthostatic hypotension can worsen cardiac ischemia.
May lower seizure threshold.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concomitant use with strong CYP3A4 inducers
contraindications.reason: Reduced paliperidone exposure and loss of efficacy.
Category C; neonatal withdrawal and EPS reported.
Excreted in breast milk; potential for infant sedation.
